economic_notes: Three government business enterprises accounted for on modified equity basis: Fishing Lake First Nation Ventures Limited Partnership (99.999% owned), Fishing Lake First Nation Ventures Ltd. (100%), and Fishing Lake Store Inc. (100%). Fishing Lake Indian Child and Family Services Inc. was fully consolidated. A new school capital construction project was a major capital activity during the year.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020, not 2024. Financial statements were signed December 18, 2020. Three GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis (FLFN Ventures LP, FLFN Ventures Ltd., Fishing Lake Store Inc.). Fishing Lake Indian Child and Family Services Inc. was fully consolidated. The Convenience Store and Gas Bar was previously consolidated but transferred to Fishing Lake Store Inc. (a GBE) during the year. A large school capital construction project drove significant deferred revenue movements (~$18.2M recognized from prior deferred balance). The TLE Trust is excluded from consolidation. Long-term debt includes CMHC mortgages (current $237,373 + long-term $3,088,175) plus RBC loans ($592,602). Total long-term debt on balance sheet = $592,602 + $3,088,175 + $237,373 current CMHC = $3,918,150 total debt obligations. Net debt position of -$1,979,293. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $13,103,855 + FNIHB $1,008,234 + CMHC $424,979 + Surrender Trust $1,267,630 + Treaty 4 Education Alliance $49,836 + SIIT $182,979 + FSIN $281444 (partially government) = approximate; osr is estimated as total revenue minus identified government transfers.
